items.title: Commits & Branches
items.description: Every PR is one branch's commits proposed for merge into another — the underlying primitives must be solid.

items.title: Merging & Rebasing
items.description: The PR merge button performs one of merge / squash / rebase-and-merge; the choice is team policy.

items.title: Git Workflows
items.description: PRs are how virtually all modern workflows (GitHub Flow, trunk-based, GitFlow) actually deliver changes.

items.title: Unit Tests
items.description: CI on a PR usually runs the test suite; failing tests block the merge button.

items.title: Communication & Collaboration
items.description: Review comments train the same clarification and disagreement skills that make day-to-day collaboration work.
